title: Passwords should not be stored in plaintext or with a fast hashing algorithm
description: Attackers who would get access to the stored passwords could reuse them without further attacks or with little additional effort. Obtaining the plaintext passwords, they could then gain unauthorized access to user accounts, potentially leading to various malicious activities. What is the potential impact? Plaintext or weakly hashed password storage poses a significant security risk to software applications.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
